title: pleopeltis wightiana
intro: Pleopeltis wightiana, also known as Wight's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Pleopeltis wightiana is often used as a decoction, dried powder, or in combination with other herbs to create teas, capsules, and tonics.
